questions.stem:
—Have you ever been to Beijing?
—Yes, I ________ there twice. It's a beautiful city.
questions.explanation: 正确答案选C。现在完成时'have been to'表示'去过某地（已经回来）'，常与表示次数的词如twice, once等连用。A选项'have gone to'表示'去了某地（还没回来）'，不符合语境；B选项一般过去时虽然语法正确，但无法与twice形成最佳搭配体现经历；D选项过去进行时表示过去正在进行的动作，与题意不符。解题技巧：看到'twice'等次数标志词，优先考虑现在完成时；区分'have been to'（去过已回）和'have gone to'（去了未回）。
questions.label: 语法题

questions.stem: The boy ________ is standing under the big tree is my cousin. He just came back from Australia.
questions.explanation: 正确答案选B。考查定语从句关系代词的选择。先行词'the boy'指人，且关系代词在从句中作主语（is standing），因此用who。A选项which用于指物；C选项whom虽然指人，但只能作宾语；D选项whose表示所属关系，在从句中作定语。解题技巧：指人用who/whom/that，指物用which/that；从句缺主语用who/which，缺宾语可用who/whom/which，有'的'含义用whose。
questions.label: 语法题

questions.stem: These old books ________ to the school library by Bosco tomorrow morning.
questions.explanation: 正确答案选C。考查一般将来时的被动语态。主语'books'与动词'return'是被动关系（书被归还），且时间状语'tomorrow morning'表示将来，因此用'will be returned'。A选项是主动语态原形；B选项是过去式，时态不符；D选项是现在进行时，且主动形式表示被动含义通常用于某些特定动词（如sell, wash等），return不适用此用法。解题口诀：被动语态be done，将来时will be done，进行时be being done。
questions.label: 语法题

questions.stem:
—Is that Mr. Wang knocking at the door?
—It ________ be him. He has gone to Shanghai for an important meeting.
questions.explanation: 正确答案选C。考查情态动词表推测的用法。根据后句'He has gone to Shanghai'（他去上海了，还没回来），可知不可能是他，表否定推测用can't（不可能）。A选项must表示肯定推测'一定是'，与语境矛盾；B选项might表示'可能是'，不确定性，但此处是确定性否定；D选项should表示'应该'，不表推测。解题技巧：肯定推测用must，否定推测用can't，不确定用might/may；注意'has gone to'表示去了未回，'has been to'表示去过已回。
questions.label: 语法题

questions.stem: The weather was terrible yesterday. It was raining heavily and the wind was blowing ________.
questions.explanation: 正确答案选A。考查副词与动词的搭配。形容风'大'或'强烈'用strongly；形容雨'大'用heavily或hard；C选项hardly意为'几乎不'；D选项quickly形容速度快，不用于形容风。固定搭配：rain heavily（下大雨），blow strongly（刮大风）。解题技巧：注意区分形近词hard（猛烈地）和hardly（几乎不）；不同自然现象用不同形容词修饰。
questions.label: 词汇题

questions.stem: As environmental volunteers, we need to ________ some good ideas to reduce plastic waste in our school.
questions.explanation: 正确答案选C。考查动词短语辨析。come up with意为'想出，提出（主意、计划等）'，常与idea/plan/solution搭配；A选项look for强调'寻找'的过程；B选项find out强调'查明，弄清楚'事实；D选项think about意为'考虑'，但不强调'提出新想法'的结果。固定搭配：come up with an idea（想出主意），find out the truth（查明真相）。
questions.label: 词汇题

questions.stem: The ________ news made all of us feel very ________ at Bosco's birthday party.
questions.explanation: 正确答案选C。考查-ing形容词和-ed形容词的用法区别。exciting意为'令人兴奋的'，修饰事物（news）；excited意为'感到兴奋的'，修饰人（us）。口诀：'-ing形容物，令人怎么样；-ed形容人，感到怎么样'。类似用法的词还有：interesting/interested, boring/bored, surprising/surprised等。
questions.label: 词汇题

questions.group_passage:
Last month, our school started a green project called "My Little Garden". Each class was given a small piece of land (土地) in the schoolyard to grow vegetables or flowers.

At first, many students thought it would be (8)_______. However, they soon realized that gardening requires a lot of work. We had to learn how to (9)_______ the soil, when to water the plants, and how to (10)_______ them from pests (害虫).

One of the biggest challenges was the weather. Two weeks ago, there was a heavy storm. Our tomato plants were badly (11)_______. Instead of giving up, we decided to build a simple shelter (遮蔽物) using bamboo sticks and plastic sheets. It took us three afternoons to finish it, but it worked well.

Now, our garden is full of life. The tomatoes are turning red, and the lettuce is growing fast. Through this project, we have learned not only gardening skills but also the importance of patience and teamwork. When we see the fruits of our labor, we feel a strong sense of (12)_______.
questions.explanation: 正确答案选C。考查语境理解和逻辑关系。根据下一句'However, they soon realized that gardening requires a lot of work'（然而，他们很快意识到园艺需要大量工作）中的转折词However可知，前后意思相反。后面说需要大量工作（难），前面应该说'容易'，因此选easy。A选项difficult（困难的）与后文逻辑重复；B选项boring（无聊的）和D选项interesting（有趣的）无法与后文形成转折对比。
questions.label: 完形填空

questions.group_passage:
Last month, our school started a green project called "My Little Garden". Each class was given a small piece of land (土地) in the schoolyard to grow vegetables or flowers.

At first, many students thought it would be (8)_______. However, they soon realized that gardening requires a lot of work. We had to learn how to (9)_______ the soil, when to water the plants, and how to (10)_______ them from pests (害虫).

One of the biggest challenges was the weather. Two weeks ago, there was a heavy storm. Our tomato plants were badly (11)_______. Instead of giving up, we decided to build a simple shelter (遮蔽物) using bamboo sticks and plastic sheets. It took us three afternoons to finish it, but it worked well.

Now, our garden is full of life. The tomatoes are turning red, and the lettuce is growing fast. Through this project, we have learned not only gardening skills but also the importance of patience and teamwork. When we see the fruits of our labor, we feel a strong sense of (12)_______.
questions.explanation: 正确答案选B。考查固定搭配和语境理解。prepare the soil意为'准备土壤/整地'，是园艺活动中的常用表达，包括松土、施肥等准备工作。A选项make the soil搭配不当；C选项clean the soil（清理土壤）不符合种植前的准备语境；D选项dig the soil（挖土）只是整地的一部分，不如prepare全面。固定搭配：prepare the soil/ground（整地）。
questions.label: 完形填空

questions.group_passage:
Last month, our school started a green project called "My Little Garden". Each class was given a small piece of land (土地) in the schoolyard to grow vegetables or flowers.

At first, many students thought it would be (8)_______. However, they soon realized that gardening requires a lot of work. We had to learn how to (9)_______ the soil, when to water the plants, and how to (10)_______ them from pests (害虫).

One of the biggest challenges was the weather. Two weeks ago, there was a heavy storm. Our tomato plants were badly (11)_______. Instead of giving up, we decided to build a simple shelter (遮蔽物) using bamboo sticks and plastic sheets. It took us three afternoons to finish it, but it worked well.

Now, our garden is full of life. The tomatoes are turning red, and the lettuce is growing fast. Through this project, we have learned not only gardening skills but also the importance of patience and teamwork. When we see the fruits of our labor, we feel a strong sense of (12)_______.
questions.explanation: 正确答案选C。考查固定搭配。protect...from...是固定搭配，意为'保护……免受……（伤害）'，此处指保护植物免受害虫侵害。A选项stop...from意为'阻止……做某事'，主语通常是人；B选项prevent...from也意为'阻止'，但语气较强，且不如protect符合'保护植物'的语境；D选项keep...from意为'阻止'或'使……远离'，但keep一般不与from搭配表示保护含义。固定搭配：protect sb/sth from danger/damage（保护某人/某物免受危险/损害）。
questions.label: 完形填空

questions.group_passage:
Last month, our school started a green project called "My Little Garden". Each class was given a small piece of land (土地) in the schoolyard to grow vegetables or flowers.

At first, many students thought it would be (8)_______. However, they soon realized that gardening requires a lot of work. We had to learn how to (9)_______ the soil, when to water the plants, and how to (10)_______ them from pests (害虫).

One of the biggest challenges was the weather. Two weeks ago, there was a heavy storm. Our tomato plants were badly (11)_______. Instead of giving up, we decided to build a simple shelter (遮蔽物) using bamboo sticks and plastic sheets. It took us three afternoons to finish it, but it worked well.

Now, our garden is full of life. The tomatoes are turning red, and the lettuce is growing fast. Through this project, we have learned not only gardening skills but also the importance of patience and teamwork. When we see the fruits of our labor, we feel a strong sense of (12)_______.
questions.explanation: 正确答案选B。考查动词辨析。damage意为'损坏，损害'，常用于描述风暴、事故等对物体造成的部分损坏，符合语境（植物被暴风雨损坏，但后文提到没有放弃，说明不是完全毁坏）。A选项hurt主要用于人或动物受伤；C选项broken强调'破碎，断裂'，通常指具体物品破裂；D选项destroyed强调'彻底毁坏'，程度过重，与后文'继续种植'矛盾。辨析：damage（部分损坏）vs destroy（彻底摧毁）。
questions.label: 完形填空

questions.group_passage:
Last month, our school started a green project called "My Little Garden". Each class was given a small piece of land (土地) in the schoolyard to grow vegetables or flowers.

At first, many students thought it would be (8)_______. However, they soon realized that gardening requires a lot of work. We had to learn how to (9)_______ the soil, when to water the plants, and how to (10)_______ them from pests (害虫).

One of the biggest challenges was the weather. Two weeks ago, there was a heavy storm. Our tomato plants were badly (11)_______. Instead of giving up, we decided to build a simple shelter (遮蔽物) using bamboo sticks and plastic sheets. It took us three afternoons to finish it, but it worked well.

Now, our garden is full of life. The tomatoes are turning red, and the lettuce is growing fast. Through this project, we have learned not only gardening skills but also the importance of patience and teamwork. When we see the fruits of our labor, we feel a strong sense of (12)_______.
questions.explanation: 正确答案选B。考查固定搭配和语境呼应。a sense of achievement意为'成就感'，与前文'the fruits of our labor'（劳动成果）形成呼应，强调通过努力获得成果后的感受。A选项pride（自豪感）虽然语义相近，但通常与in搭配（pride in sth）；C选项success（成功）一般不与a sense of搭配；D选项happiness（快乐）过于泛泛，不如achievement准确表达'通过努力获得'的含义。固定搭配：a sense of achievement（成就感），a sense of pride（自豪感）。
questions.label: 完形填空

questions.group_passage:
In recent years, "phubbing" (低头症) has become a common problem among teenagers. This word comes from "phone" and "snubbing" (冷落). It describes the habit of ignoring people around you because you are using your smartphone.

A recent study shows that teenagers spend an average of 7 hours a day on their phones. This has raised concerns among parents and teachers. Dr. Li, a psychologist (心理学家), explains that excessive (过度的) phone use can affect our relationships and mental health. "When you look at your phone instead of talking to your friends, you send a message that they are not important," says Dr. Li.

However, phones are not completely bad. They help us stay connected with friends, access (获取) information quickly, and even learn new things. The key is to find a balance. Dr. Li suggests setting specific "phone-free" times, such as during meals or before bedtime.

Last month, Class 8A tried a "Digital Detox (排毒) Week". Students agreed to use their phones only for necessary calls and messages. At first, many felt anxious without their phones. But by the end of the week, they reported better sleep and more face-to-face conversations with classmates.

"At first, I didn't know what to do with my free time," said Bosco, a student from Class 8A. "But then I started reading books and playing basketball with friends. I realized there are many interesting things offline."
questions.stem: What does the word "phubbing" mean according to the passage?
options: A. A new type of smartphone.
B. The habit of ignoring people around you because of using phones.
C. A popular game among teenagers.
D. The skill of communicating with friends online.
questions.explanation: 正确答案选B。细节理解题。根据第一段最后一句'It describes the habit of ignoring people around you because you are using your smartphone.'可知，phubbing指的是因为使用手机而忽视周围人的习惯。A选项说是一种新型智能手机，C选项说是一种流行游戏，D选项说是在线交流技巧，均与原文描述不符。解题技巧：细节题要准确定位原文，注意题干中的专有名词（phubbing），快速扫描原文找到对应解释句。
questions.label: 阅读-细节理解

questions.group_passage:
In recent years, "phubbing" (低头症) has become a common problem among teenagers. This word comes from "phone" and "snubbing" (冷落). It describes the habit of ignoring people around you because you are using your smartphone.

A recent study shows that teenagers spend an average of 7 hours a day on their phones. This has raised concerns among parents and teachers. Dr. Li, a psychologist (心理学家), explains that excessive (过度的) phone use can affect our relationships and mental health. "When you look at your phone instead of talking to your friends, you send a message that they are not important," says Dr. Li.

However, phones are not completely bad. They help us stay connected with friends, access (获取) information quickly, and even learn new things. The key is to find a balance. Dr. Li suggests setting specific "phone-free" times, such as during meals or before bedtime.

Last month, Class 8A tried a "Digital Detox (排毒) Week". Students agreed to use their phones only for necessary calls and messages. At first, many felt anxious without their phones. But by the end of the week, they reported better sleep and more face-to-face conversations with classmates.

"At first, I didn't know what to do with my free time," said Bosco, a student from Class 8A. "But then I started reading books and playing basketball with friends. I realized there are many interesting things offline."
questions.stem: What does the underlined word "access" in Paragraph 3 probably mean in Chinese?
questions.explanation: 正确答案选B。词义猜测题。根据上下文'access information quickly'（快速地……信息），结合常识，手机帮助我们快速'获取'信息。A选项存储（save/store）、C选项分享（share）、D选项删除（delete）均不如'获取'符合语境。解题技巧：通过上下文逻辑和常识猜测词义；access常见含义为'进入'或'获取/使用（信息、设施等）'，如internet access（互联网接入）。
questions.label: 阅读-词义猜测

questions.group_passage:
In recent years, "phubbing" (低头症) has become a common problem among teenagers. This word comes from "phone" and "snubbing" (冷落). It describes the habit of ignoring people around you because you are using your smartphone.

A recent study shows that teenagers spend an average of 7 hours a day on their phones. This has raised concerns among parents and teachers. Dr. Li, a psychologist (心理学家), explains that excessive (过度的) phone use can affect our relationships and mental health. "When you look at your phone instead of talking to your friends, you send a message that they are not important," says Dr. Li.

However, phones are not completely bad. They help us stay connected with friends, access (获取) information quickly, and even learn new things. The key is to find a balance. Dr. Li suggests setting specific "phone-free" times, such as during meals or before bedtime.

Last month, Class 8A tried a "Digital Detox (排毒) Week". Students agreed to use their phones only for necessary calls and messages. At first, many felt anxious without their phones. But by the end of the week, they reported better sleep and more face-to-face conversations with classmates.

"At first, I didn't know what to do with my free time," said Bosco, a student from Class 8A. "But then I started reading books and playing basketball with friends. I realized there are many interesting things offline."
questions.stem: What can we infer from the last paragraph?
options: A. Bosco regretted joining the Digital Detox Week.
B. Bosco found offline activities more boring than using phones.
C. Bosco benefited from the activity and discovered new interests.
D. Bosco decided to stop using his phone forever.
questions.explanation: 正确答案选C。推理判断题。根据最后一段Bosco的话：起初不知道空闲时间该做什么，但后来开始读书、打篮球，意识到线下有很多有趣的事情。可推断出他从活动中受益并发现了新的兴趣爱好。A选项'后悔参加'与原文态度相反；B选项'觉得线下活动更无聊'与原文'many interesting things'矛盾；D选项'决定永远不用手机'过于绝对，文中只说发现线下活动有趣，没说永远不用手机。解题技巧：推理题要基于原文信息合理推断，不能过度推断，也不能选择与原文相反或绝对的选项。
questions.label: 阅读-推理判断

questions.group_passage:
In recent years, "phubbing" (低头症) has become a common problem among teenagers. This word comes from "phone" and "snubbing" (冷落). It describes the habit of ignoring people around you because you are using your smartphone.

A recent study shows that teenagers spend an average of 7 hours a day on their phones. This has raised concerns among parents and teachers. Dr. Li, a psychologist (心理学家), explains that excessive (过度的) phone use can affect our relationships and mental health. "When you look at your phone instead of talking to your friends, you send a message that they are not important," says Dr. Li.

However, phones are not completely bad. They help us stay connected with friends, access (获取) information quickly, and even learn new things. The key is to find a balance. Dr. Li suggests setting specific "phone-free" times, such as during meals or before bedtime.

Last month, Class 8A tried a "Digital Detox (排毒) Week". Students agreed to use their phones only for necessary calls and messages. At first, many felt anxious without their phones. But by the end of the week, they reported better sleep and more face-to-face conversations with classmates.

"At first, I didn't know what to do with my free time," said Bosco, a student from Class 8A. "But then I started reading books and playing basketball with friends. I realized there are many interesting things offline."
questions.stem: What is the best title for this passage?
options: B. Phubbing: A Modern Problem and Solutions
C. How to Use Phones at School
questions.explanation: 正确答案选B。主旨大意题/最佳标题题。文章第一段引入phubbing（低头症）这一现代问题，第二段讲其危害，第三段讲如何平衡使用，第四、五段讲具体解决尝试（数字排毒周）。因此B选项'Phubbing: A Modern Problem and Solutions'（低头症：一个现代问题及解决方案）最能概括全文。A选项'智能手机历史'只在开头提及，非重点；C选项'如何在校使用手机'过于片面；D选项'青少年生活'范围过大。解题技巧：最佳标题要涵盖全文主要内容，既要有问题描述也要有解决建议，不能过于宽泛或片面。
questions.label: 阅读-主旨大意

questions.group_passage:
Waste sorting (垃圾分类) has become an important topic in our daily life. It helps protect the environment and save resources. (17)_______

First, we should learn to separate waste into different categories (类别). Usually, there are four types: recyclables, hazardous waste (有害垃圾), kitchen waste, and other waste. (18)_______ For example, plastic bottles and paper belong to recyclables, while batteries are hazardous waste.

Second, we need to develop good habits. (19)_______ Before throwing something away, stop and think which bin it should go into. This small action can make a big difference.

(20)_______ Schools and communities are organizing various activities to promote waste sorting. By working together, we can create a cleaner and greener future.
options: A. However, many people find it difficult to start.
B. Here are some simple steps we can follow.
C. In addition, we should encourage our family and friends to join us.
D. It may seem troublesome at first, but it will become easier with practice.
E. Knowing the categories is the first step to proper sorting.
questions.explanation: 正确答案选B。考查段落衔接和总分结构。空前讲垃圾分类的重要性，空后分点阐述（First... Second...），因此此处需要一个总起句引出具体步骤。B选项'Here are some simple steps we can follow'（以下是一些我们可以遵循的简单步骤）起到承上启下的作用。A选项表转折，与后文逻辑不符；C选项是补充建议，应放在段末；D选项是让步状语从句，不适合作为总起句；E选项具体讲分类，应放在First之后。
questions.label: 短文填空

questions.group_passage:
Waste sorting (垃圾分类) has become an important topic in our daily life. It helps protect the environment and save resources. (17)_______

First, we should learn to separate waste into different categories (类别). Usually, there are four types: recyclables, hazardous waste (有害垃圾), kitchen waste, and other waste. (18)_______ For example, plastic bottles and paper belong to recyclables, while batteries are hazardous waste.

Second, we need to develop good habits. (19)_______ Before throwing something away, stop and think which bin it should go into. This small action can make a big difference.

(20)_______ Schools and communities are organizing various activities to promote waste sorting. By working together, we can create a cleaner and greener future.
options: A. However, many people find it difficult to start.
B. Here are some simple steps we can follow.
C. In addition, we should encourage our family and friends to join us.
D. It may seem troublesome at first, but it will become easier with practice.
E. Knowing the categories is the first step to proper sorting.
questions.explanation: 正确答案选E。考查逻辑衔接。空前提到'four types'（四种类型），空后举例说明哪些属于可回收物，哪些是有害垃圾，因此此处应强调了解分类的重要性。E选项'Knowing the categories is the first step to proper sorting'（了解类别是正确分类的第一步）承上启下，既呼应上文的categories，又引出下文的例子。A、B、C、D选项均与'举例说明具体分类'的语境不符。
questions.label: 短文填空

questions.group_passage:

Second, we need to develop good habits. (19)_______ Before throwing something away, stop and think which bin it should go into. This small action can make a big difference.

B. Here are some simple steps we can follow.

D. It may seem troublesome at first, but it will become easier with practice.
E. Knowing the categories is the first step to proper sorting.
questions.explanation: 正确答案选D。考查逻辑关系和语境理解。空前说'我们需要养成良好的习惯'，空后说'扔垃圾前先停下来思考该放进哪个桶'，D选项'It may seem troublesome at first, but it will become easier with practice'（起初可能看起来麻烦，但练习后会变得更容易）承上启下，解释了养成习惯的过程，且与后文的'stop and think'形成呼应（停下来思考就是troublesome的体现）。A选项表转折但过于负面；C选项是另一个要点；E选项与分类知识相关，此处讲习惯养成。
questions.label: 短文填空

questions.group_passage:
Waste sorting (垃圾分类) has become an important topic in our daily life. It helps protect the environment and save resources. (17)_______

First, we should learn to separate waste into different categories (类别). Usually, there are four types: recyclables, hazardous waste (有害垃圾), kitchen waste, and other waste. (18)_______ For example, plastic bottles and paper belong to recyclables, while batteries are hazardous waste.

Second, we need to develop good habits. (19)_______ Before throwing something away, stop and think which bin it should go into. This small action can make a big difference.

(20)_______ Schools and communities are organizing various activities to promote waste sorting. By working together, we can create a cleaner and greener future.
options: A. However, many people find it difficult to start.
B. Here are some simple steps we can follow.
C. In addition, we should encourage our family and friends to join us.
D. It may seem troublesome at first, but it will become easier with practice.
E. Knowing the categories is the first step to proper sorting.
questions.explanation: 正确答案选C。考查段落间衔接和递进关系。最后一段讲学校和社会组织活动推广垃圾分类，强调'By working together'（通过共同努力），因此此处需要一个表示'共同参与'的过渡句。C选项'In addition, we should encourage our family and friends to join us'（此外，我们应该鼓励家人和朋友加入我们）符合逻辑，In addition表示补充递进，且'family and friends'与'working together'呼应。A选项转折；B选项总起；D选项讲习惯养成；E选项讲分类知识，均不符合最后一段的总结升华语境。
questions.label: 短文填空
